introduction: In this presentation, we will discuss the English proficiency trends among our seasonal staff over the past year. This data is crucial to understand as it impacts our customer service and overall business performance.

subsections.heading: Seasonal Staff Recruitment Trends
subsections.content:
• Increased focus on recruiting from local communities to foster a sense of familiarity in stores
• Augmented reality simulations used in interviews to assess candidates' customer service skills
• In 2025, 40% of seasonal staff were recruited through digital channels, reflecting a shift in recruitment strategy
• Partnership with local universities for work-study programs led to a 20% increase in applications
• Implemented a new recruitment software in July 2025 to streamline the hiring process
• Seasonal staff turnover rate decreased by 5% in 2025 compared to 2024
• The average time to hire for seasonal staff reduced from 14 days in 2024 to 10 days in 2025
subsections.justification: While these trends provide context on staff recruitment, they don't address English test failures or performance changes.

subsections.heading: English Proficiency Test Performance
subsections.content:
• Introduced new language training programs for staff in January 2025
• Improved English proficiency test by introducing AI-graded essays in February 2025
• Noticed a 15% increase in test pass rates for full-time employees in Q2 2025
• Launched a partnership with an online language learning platform in April 2025
• Periodic language proficiency assessments were introduced for all staff in June 2025
• Conducted a company-wide English language proficiency survey in July 2025
• Introduced a new language support policy for non-native English speakers in August 2025
subsections.justification: These points relate to English proficiency but don't specifically address seasonal staff test failures between December 2024 and September 2025.

subsections.heading: Impact on Customer Service Metrics
subsections.content:
• Customer satisfaction scores increased by 10% from Q1 to Q3 2025
• Noticed a 5% decrease in customer complaints related to staff communication in 2025
• Average checkout time reduced by 2 minutes in 2025 due to improved communication skills
• Online reviews indicating 'friendly staff' increased by 25% in 2025
• Staff upselling success rate increased by 15% in Q2 2025
• Return shoppers proportion increased by 8% in 2025, possibly due to improved customer service
• Launched a new customer feedback system in May 2025 to better gauge customer satisfaction
subsections.justification: While these metrics are influenced by staff performance, they don't specifically address the English test failure rates of seasonal staff.

conclusion: Our analysis shows a need for improved English language training for our seasonal staff to enhance customer service. Moving forward, we recommend implementing a more comprehensive language training program during onboarding.
